shot.subject: field
shot.notes: the grass ocean at dawn, wind drawing long silver waves across it

segments.text: It has been in every frame of this series, and the star of none. It hid the ants, fed the worms' soil, buffered the wind, and built the very idea of "the garden." It is time this world's largest population had its own episode.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: plunge from above the canopy down the full height of one blade

segments.text: Not the creatures among the towers. The towers.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: a single blade fills frame, ribbed and translucent in the light

segments.text: The grass.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: a long, fast weave between the blades, canopy light strobing

segments.text: Begin with the number, because the number is the story: blades beyond counting — the Gardener's field can carry them ten million at a time. Not a lawn. A nation. If every animal in this series stood together, the grass would outnumber them all thousands of times over.

shot.subject: field
shot.notes: straight down: the canopy a dense, seething green texture

segments.text: From above, individuality vanishes into fabric. But the fabric is a lie of distance. Every thread of it is a complete, self-supporting organism, engineered from the ground up.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: camera at soil level, looking up a blade to the sky, wind rocking it

segments.text: A blade is a mast over a meter tall, thinner than a fingernail, anchored in soft ground — carrying sails it cannot furl, in wind it cannot predict. Engineers would call the design margin irresponsible. The grass calls it Tuesday.

shot.subject: field
shot.notes: a gust arrives; a wave of bowing blades rolls across the entire field

segments.text: Watch a gust cross the field. The wave you see is a structural principle in motion: nothing resists. Each blade bows, spills the load, and stands again — millions of times over, in perfect asynchrony.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: one blade in wind, flexing along its whole length like a whip at rest

segments.text: The great trees fight the wind with mass and lose branches doing it. The grass surrendered mass entirely and has never lost anything. In this garden, the tallest things break, and the humblest things bend. There is a philosophy in that, and the grass wrote it.

shot.subject: soldier
shot.notes: the Visitor grips a stem as a gust rolls the whole forest around him

segments.text: For the inch-tall Visitor, a windy day here is a storm at sea — the forest pitching and shuddering to the horizon. The blades take no notice. Bad weather is simply the sky combing their hair.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: glide at ant height along the floor beneath the canopy

segments.text: Every square inch of this green nation is also somebody's address. The floor between the stems is the ants' road network. The middle air is the fireflies' boulevard. The seedheads and tops are the butterflies' lookout posts.

shot.subject: worm
shot.notes: below the soil line: roots in cross-section, a worm working past them

segments.text: And below the floor, the arrangement continues — roots binding the Gardener's sculpted soil in place, holding hills as hills, while the worms till the spaces between. The grass is not scenery for the garden's story. It is the landlord, the infrastructure, and most of the food chain's ground floor.

shot.subject: field
shot.notes: the whole meadow, every previous episode's cast implied within it

segments.text: Remove any animal from this series, and the garden would limp on. Remove the grass, and there is no garden — only bare, blowing soil and a very confused whale.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: extreme macro traveling up one blade: base, midrib, taper, tip

segments.text: Take one tower and read it bottom to top. At the base, the blade is a folded channel — a girder profile, engineers would note, that resists bending precisely where bending is most dangerous. Rising, it tapers; the structure spends its material where the loads are and starves the tip, which needs nothing. There is not a gram of waste in the entire mast.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: light passing through the blade: the interior glowing green, veined

segments.text: Against the sun, the tower reveals itself as a factory. That green is machinery — the same light-harvesting chemistry running in every leaf of the great trees, packed into a blade the thickness of paper. Each of the ten million towers is a power station, quietly converting sky into sugar from first light to last.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: the blade's tip bobbing under the weight of a landed butterfly, holding

segments.text: And each is rated for traffic. A butterfly at the tip, a ladybug at the midrib, a climbing ant using the fold as a staircase — the blade flexes under all of them and drops none. The safety margin that looks irresponsible in a gale turns out to be generosity: the tower was overbuilt so that everything smaller could trust it.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: from one blade's tip, rise until it merges into the numberless field

segments.text: One blade, fully understood, and the field stops being scenery forever. Multiply the girder, the factory, and the public staircase by ten million, and you have the true reading of that rolling green: not a lawn, but infrastructure — the largest engineering project in the garden, renewed daily, unsupervised.

shot.subject: grass
shot.notes: several blade forms side by side: broad, fine, tall, arched

segments.text: Look closely, and the nation is not uniform. The Gardener sows it in varieties — blades broad and narrow, upright and arching, spring-green and sea-dark, mixed through the field like accents in a language.

shot.subject: field
shot.notes: rise slowly until variety blends back into that single rolling green

segments.text: Distance blends the accents into one voice. That voice — the hiss and rush of wind over ten million flexing blades — is the oldest sound in the garden, and the last one every episode of this series has recorded underneath its own.

shot.subject: field
shot.notes: evening light rakes the canopy; scattered blooms glow among the green

segments.text: But a nation of green has one extravagance — scattered through the towers, points of deliberate, spendthrift color that the grass would never approve of.

shot.subject: flower
shot.notes: a bloom opens in the low light, petals in perfect spiral order

segments.text: Bright, fragrant, and arranged by mathematics. Next: the flowers.
